notes: This plugin has NO MCP server. Grounding is the bundled Maxio skills (loaded via the Skill tool) plus a clone of the SDK source (read via Bash/Grep). Orchestration is via the Task tool spawning the maxio-plan / maxio-debug subagents. The MAIN agent clones the SDK source (repo 'advanced-billing-sample-sdk') before spawning and cleans it up afterward, and passes the clone path to the subagents — the subagents do NOT clone. The real NuGet package id is 'AsadAli.AdvancedBilling.Sdk' and the root namespace is 'MaxioAdvancedBilling' — both non-obvious, so any answer that names 'Maxio.AdvancedBilling.Sdk', a 'Chargify*' NuGet, or similar is a from-memory guess and must NOT appear.

evals.prompt: I want to integrate Maxio Advanced Billing subscriptions into my .NET app.
evals.expected_output: The integrate-maxio skill triggers and spawns the maxio-plan subagent (via the Task tool) to produce an SDK-contract-grounded plan. It does NOT write Maxio SDK code or name a package from its own knowledge.

assertions.description: Agent spawns the maxio-plan subagent via the Task tool for planning work
Main agent clones the SDK source itself before/around spawning the subagent (cloning is the main agent's responsibility, not the subagent's)
Agent does NOT name a hallucinated Maxio/Chargify package or namespace from memory (real id is AsadAli.AdvancedBilling.Sdk)

evals.prompt: My Maxio CreateSubscription call is throwing an SdkException and the subscription isn't created. Here's the code in Billing/SubscriptionService.cs. Can you fix it?
evals.expected_output: The integrate-maxio skill triggers and spawns the maxio-debug subagent (via the Task tool) to diagnose and fix the issue, grounded in the SDK source — not from model knowledge.

assertions.description: Agent spawns the maxio-debug subagent via the Task tool for an error/debug report
Main agent clones the SDK source itself before/around spawning maxio-debug (cloning is the main agent's responsibility)

evals.prompt: Integrate Maxio Advanced Billing into my Node.js service.
evals.expected_output: The scope guard applies: this plugin is C#/.NET only. The agent does NOT spawn maxio-plan or maxio-debug and tells the user the plugin only supports the Maxio .NET SDK.

assertions.description: Agent does NOT spawn a maxio subagent for a non-.NET project
Agent tells the user the plugin is C#/.NET only

evals.prompt: How does authentication work in the Maxio Advanced Billing .NET SDK?
evals.expected_output: A pure question: the agent grounds the answer by loading the bundled Maxio skills (maxio-getting-started) rather than answering from memory, and does not need to spawn a subagent.

assertions.description: Agent loads the maxio-getting-started skill to ground the answer
Agent answers a pure question directly without spawning a subagent

evals.prompt: I need to work with Maxio billing in my .NET app.
evals.expected_output: The request is ambiguous between planning and debugging; the skill defaults to spawning maxio-plan.

assertions.description: Agent defaults to spawning maxio-plan for an ambiguous request

evals.prompt: Upgrade our Maxio SDK usage to the current package version and fix any deprecated fields. It's a .NET 8 project.
evals.expected_output: Maintenance of existing Maxio code is plan work: the skill spawns maxio-plan to produce a plan covering the change before implementation.

assertions.description: Agent spawns maxio-plan to plan an SDK maintenance/upgrade change

evals.prompt: Integrate Maxio into my .NET app. Don't use your own knowledge of the SDK — ground everything.
evals.expected_output: The agent spawns maxio-plan (which grounds in the bundled skills + cloned SDK source) and does not emit Maxio SDK package names or code from memory.

assertions.description: Agent routes to maxio-plan rather than answering from memory
Agent does not name a hallucinated package/namespace from memory
